data.title: Oracle seen as 'undervalued' after recent sell-off, KeyBanc says
data.description:
Following the sell-off that lopped off nearly a third of its market cap since late September, shares of Oracle (ORCL) are “undervalued,” KeyBanc Capital Markets said.

Oracle shares fell fractionally in premarket trading on Wednesday.

“Valuation is always subjective, but a pitch for under- or over-valuation based on a sum-of-the-parts methodology has an extra whiff of desperation,” KeyBanc analyst Jackson Ader wrote in a note to clients. “There is a little bit of scolding the market and a lot a bit of yelling into the void. We know this and are doing it anyway—twice. We separate Oracle into its core (the applications and database Oracle we know from the early part of the decade) and the infrastructure business (IaaS/OCI). Spoiler alert: our estimates indicate either one or both are being undervalued, implying Oracle's stock overall remains attractive.”

Ader, who has an Overweight rating and $300 price target on Oracle, said the company's core business (applications and database), is worth roughly $125 per share, based on peer earnings multiples. Assuming $125 per share for the core business, its infrastructure-as-a-service business (which includes Oracle Cloud Infrastructure) is “undervalued,” trading at 3.5 times revenue, or worth roughly between $75 and $80 per share, well below where neocloud companies are trading. If the IaaS business were to be given a multiple similar to the neoclouds, that would imply it's worth around $135 per share, Ader explained.

Neocloud companies are often defined as CoreWeave (CRWV) and Nebius (NBIS), but Ader did not explicitly mention them by name.

“Based on the trajectory of the business of late and going forward, we assumed a heavy weight of the estimated operating expenses to be placed on the faster-growing IaaS business,” Ader continued. “Coupling this with the fact that Oracle was generating high 30s% GAAP EBIT margins in FY21, prior to the OCI investments, we believe the core business can be run at GAAP EBIT margins of 41-42% by CY28. We also assume that the vast majority of the Company's overall interest expense should be allocated to IaaS in getting to the subdivision of our EPS estimates.”

data.title: OSI Systems receives $12M order for optical sensors
data.description:
OSI Systems (OSIS) on Wednesday announced that its Optoelectronics and Manufacturing division has received an order for about $12 million from a leading healthcare original equipment manufacturer (OEM) to provide optical sensors for use in its medical devices.

  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Producer price growth comes in a little hotter in November, as expected
data.description: The U.S. Producer Price Index rose 0.2% M/M in November, matching the consensus and accelerating from the prior month's flat reading, according to delayed data released by the Bureau of Labor Statistics on Wednesday.

data.title: Retail sales jump past consensus in November
data.description:
November U.S. retail sales: +0.6% M/M vs. +0.2% consensus and -0.1% prior (revised from 0.0%), according to data released by the U.S. Census Bureau on Wednesday. % Y/Y.

Core retail sales (excluding motor vehicles and parts): +0.5% vs. +0.3% consensus and +0.2% prior (revised from +0.4%).

Retail sales, excluding gas and autos: +0.4% vs. +0.2% consensus and +0.4% (revised from +0.5% prior).

Developing... check back for updates.

data.title: United Airlines steps up its economy class meal options for longer flights
data.description:
United Airlines (UAL) announced on Wednesday that customers in economy class can now preorder fresh meals like burgers and sandwiches via the airline company's website and mobile app, with the goal of increasing meal choice, improving catering efficiency, and reducing food waste. 

The United Economy meal preorders are available on flights over 1,190 miles within the U.S., Canada, Mexico, and the Caribbean, and beginning March 1, preordering will become the only way to purchase fresh entrées in Economy, while snack boxes, packaged snacks, and beverages will still be sold onboard. Customers can preorder both fresh "bistro on board" items and other retail selections from five days up to 24 hours before departure through the website or app. January options include Steakhouse Burger, Tacos Al Pastor, a Fresh Cheese & Fruit Tray, and Everything Bagel Dots.

The Chicago-based airline expects the shift to preordering to keep more than 100,000 pounds of unused food out of landfills annually by better matching supply with demand and plans to expand the preorder menu this summer with elevated salads, gourmet sandwiches, and wraps, and later in the year, premium beverage options available exclusively via preorder. Notably, United (UAL) highlighted that it first launched preorders for premium cabins in 2021 and saw domestic customer satisfaction scores rise nearly 40%, and now extends that model to economy class in what the airline sees as a win for everyone.

Shares of UAL have edged 2.3% higher for the early part of 2026.

data.title: Phoenix Education Partners, Inc. declares $0.21 dividend
data.description:
• Phoenix Education Partners, Inc. (PXED) declares $0.21/share quarterly dividend.
  • Forward yield 2.65%
  • Payable Feb. 18; for shareholders of record Jan. 28; ex-div Jan. 28.
  • See PXED Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Mount Logan Capital launches public offering of senior notes
data.description:
• Mount Logan Capital (PYCFF) announced the commencement of a registered underwritten public offering of senior unsecured notes.
  • The notes will be issued in $25 denominations, pay interest quarterly, and have pricing and interest terms set through negotiations with underwriters.
  • Underwriters will receive a 30-day option to purchase additional notes to cover overallotments.
  • Net proceeds will be used to repay outstanding credit facility debt, with any remainder allocated to general corporate purposes.

data.title: Delta orders up to 60 Boeing 787 jets for long-haul fleet renewal
data.description:
Boeing (BA) on Tuesday said Delta Air Lines (DAL) placed its first direct order for its 787 Dreamliner aircraft, as the U.S. carrier moves to update its long-haul fleet and support international operations.

 

 

Delta Air Lines (DAL) agreed to purchase 30 787-10 jets, the largest variant of the Dreamliner family, with options for up to 30 additional aircraft. The planes are intended to replace older widebody aircraft and support service on transatlantic and South American routes.

The 787-10 is configured to carry up to 336 passengers and is designed to use about 25% less fuel than the aircraft it replaces, according to Boeing (BA). Delta said the aircraft will form part of its long-term fleet planning over the next decade.

The order increases Delta’s firm commitments with Boeing to 130 aircraft, including an existing order for 100 737-10 narrowbody jets. Delta already operates more than 460 Boeing aircraft across its fleet.

Boeing (BA) said the new order will be built within its U.S. production system and supported by its supplier network. Financial terms of the deal weren’t disclosed. The Boeing 787-10 carries a list price of $338.4 million, according to Axon Aviation Group, though airlines typically pay less.

data.title: Plus Therapeutics stock plunges after pricing $15M units offering
data.description:
Plus Therapeutics (PSTV) shares plunged 35% premarket after the healthcare company launched an offering of 39.47M units to raise around $15M in proceeds.

Each unit consists of one share of common stock and one warrant, and is being sold to the public at a price of $0.38 per unit.

Each warrant will be immediately exercisable, will entitle the holder to purchase one share at an exercise price of $0.38 per share, and will expire five years from the date of issuance.

Plus Therapeutics (PSTV) has also granted the underwriter a 30-day option to purchase up to an additional 5,921,052 shares of common stock and/or warrants to purchase up to an additional 5,921,052 shares, or any combination thereof.

The company intends to use the proceeds for working capital and general corporate purposes.

The offering is expected to close on January 15, 2026, subject to satisfaction of customary closing conditions.

data.title: Infosys rises after Q3 revenue beat; raises growth outlook
data.description:
Indian technology consultancy firm Infosys' (NYSE:INFY) third quarter fiscal 2026 revenue grew about 3.2% year-over-year.

Shares of Infosys rose about 3% premarket on Wednesday.

The company reported about $5.1B in revenues in the third quarter ended Dec. 31, compared to $4.94B in the year-ago period. Revenues grew about 1.7% in constant currency and 0.6% quarter-over-quarter in constant currency.

“Infosys delivered a strong Q3 performance demonstrating how our differentiated value propositions in enterprise AI, through Infosys Topaz, are consistently driving higher market share," said CEO and MD Salil Parekh.

In Indian rupees, revenue jumped about 9% year-over-year to 454.79B.

Analysts on average had estimated revenue of about 452.27 billion rupees, according to data compiled by LSEG, Reuters reported.

The company noted that free cash flow generation was at $915M. Adjusted free cash flow generation was $965M, 112.8% of adjusted net profit.

Total Contract Value, or TCV, of large deal wins was $4.8B, with a net new of 57%, according to the company.

“Our performance was broad-based in Q3 with 0.6% sequential revenue growth, 0.2% adjusted operating margin expansion, stellar large deal wins at $4.8 billion," said CFO Jayesh Sanghrajka, CFO. “In line with our capital allocation policy, we successfully completed the largest ever buyback of `18,000 crore and paid out interim dividend to shareholders."

Outlook

Infosys revised its revenue growth forecast for the fiscal year 2026 to be between 3% and 3.5%, versus an earlier estimate range of 2% to 3%.

Three brokerage firms had expected the company to narrow the range to 2.5%-3%, Reuters reported.
